description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §5.5/§5.6 define an EventWal that owns the active segment per namespace and rotates by size/age. Current code opens a new SegmentWriter for every local mutation and every replication ingest batch, so each event/batch becomes its own segment and rotation logic is effectively bypassed. This contradicts the WAL design and inflates segment count + fsync dir overhead.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/executor.rs:225–255 creates SegmentWriter::open for each mutation (no reuse).
- src/daemon/core.rs:1009–1033 (ingest_remote_batch) creates SegmentWriter::open per batch.
- There is no EventWal struct managing active segments in src/daemon/wal/mod.rs.

**Why this violates plan**
Plan requires a long-lived EventWal that appends to the current segment, rotates when size/age thresholds are hit, and only fsyncs directories on rotation. Opening a new segment per event defeats these invariants.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Introduce EventWal (per store) that keeps active SegmentWriter per namespace.
- [ ] Use EventWal::append in executor + replication ingest; reuse active segment until rotation triggers.
- [ ] Rotation honors max_segment_bytes/max_segment_age and only then creates new segment + dir fsync.
- [ ] Tests cover multi-append into a single segment and rotation thresholds.
title: Event WAL uses SegmentWriter per append; no persistent EventWal/segment reuse

acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Unit test: WalDepStore with empty dots produces DepStore with no values.\n- [ ] Unit test: WalDepStore with mixed empty/non-empty dots preserves only non-empty entries.
description:
**Problem**
WalDepStore::into_dep_store inserts entries with empty dot sets, which violates OrSet invariants and can create phantom membership.

**Files**: src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs, src/core/orset.rs
design: **Design**\nSkip entries with empty dot sets during conversion, or normalize through OrSet constructor that prunes empties. Ensure resulting DepStore contains only keys with non-empty dot sets. Add a regression test with empty-dot entries.
title: WalDepStore conversion must not create empty-dot entries
parse_legacy_state double-inserts label_store for state entries in git/wire.rs

description:
**Problem**
Privatizing `beads-rs` internals in `beads-rs-gou3.6` surfaced dead-code warnings in `crates/beads-rs/src/telemetry.rs`. The module still defines tracing record structs and schema constants that are no longer consumed through the public library surface.

**Where**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/telemetry.rs`
- warning output from `cargo check -p beads-rs` and `just dylint` after `beads-rs-gou3.6`

**Why It Matters**
The boundary cleanup is correct, but leaving newly-dead telemetry surface behind makes the crate noisy and weakens local reasoning about what telemetry APIs are actually live.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `crates/beads-rs/src/telemetry.rs` no longer emits new dead-code warnings caused by the private-module cutover
- [ ] Any telemetry types/constants that remain are justified by real internal use or explicitly documented
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs` stays green

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/telemetry.rs`
title: Trim dead telemetry surface after beads-rs boundary privatization
